description: Domain architecture comparison visualization: squid ADAR2 vs human ADAR2 vs human ADAT1, showing the mis-annotation
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as patches
import numpy as np

fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(14, 10), gridspec_kw={'height_ratios': [1.2, 1.2, 1.2, 1.5]})

# Color scheme
dsrbd_color = '#4CAF50'  # green
deaminase_color = '#2196F3'  # blue
deaminase_adat_color = '#FF9800'  # orange for ADAT
backbone_color = '#E0E0E0'  # grey

def draw_protein(ax, name, length, domains, y=0.5, height=0.6, label_offset=0):
    """Draw a protein with domains"""
    # Draw backbone
    ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((0, y-height/2), length, height, 
                                         boxstyle="round,pad=2", 
                                         facecolor=backbone_color, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1))
    
    for domain in domains:
        start, end, color, label = domain
        ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((start, y-height/2+0.05), end-start, height-0.1,
                                             boxstyle="round,pad=1",
                                             facecolor=color, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.9))
        ax.text((start+end)/2, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color='white')
    
    ax.set_xlim(-50, max(800, length+50))
    ax.set_ylim(-0.1, 1.1)
    ax.set_ylabel(name, fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', rotation=0, labelpad=120, va='center')
    ax.set_xlabel('')
    ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines['left'].set_visible(False)
    ax.set_yticks([])
    ax.set_xticks(range(0, max(800, length+50), 100))
    ax.tick_params(axis='x', labelsize=8)

# Squid ADAR2
draw_protein(axes[0], "Squid ADAR2\n(C1JAR3)\n786 aa", 786, [
    (57, 106, dsrbd_color, 'dsRBD1'),
    (164, 230, dsrbd_color, 'dsRBD2'),
    (315, 364, dsrbd_color, 'dsRBD3'),
    (434, 761, deaminase_color, 'Adenosine Deaminase\n(A-to-I editase)'),
])
# Mark active site
axes[0].annotate('CHAE\n(457)', xy=(457, 0.15), fontsize=7, color='red', fontweight='bold', ha='center')
axes[0].plot(457, 0.22, 'v', color='red', markersize=8)

# Human ADAR2
draw_protein(axes[1], "Human ADAR2\n(P78563)\n741 aa", 741, [
    (78, 144, dsrbd_color, 'dsRBD1'),
    (231, 298, dsrbd_color, 'dsRBD2'),
    (370, 737, deaminase_color, 'Adenosine Deaminase\n(A-to-I editase)'),
])
axes[1].annotate('CHAE\n(394)', xy=(394, 0.15), fontsize=7, color='red', fontweight='bold', ha='center')
axes[1].plot(394, 0.22, 'v', color='red', markersize=8)

# Human ADAT1
draw_protein(axes[2], "Human ADAT1\n(Q9BUB4)\n502 aa", 502, [
    (63, 501, deaminase_adat_color, 'Adenosine Deaminase\n(tRNA-specific, A37)'),
])
axes[2].annotate('No CHAE\nmotif', xy=(89, 0.15), fontsize=7, color='red', fontweight='bold', ha='center')
axes[2].plot(89, 0.22, 'v', color='red', markersize=8)

# Legend and summary
axes[3].axis('off')
legend_elements = [
    patches.Patch(facecolor=dsrbd_color, edgecolor='black', label='dsRNA Binding Domain (dsRBD)'),
    patches.Patch(facecolor=deaminase_color, edgecolor='black', label='ADAR-type Deaminase (mRNA substrate)'),
    patches.Patch(facecolor=deaminase_adat_color, edgecolor='black', label='ADAT-type Deaminase (tRNA substrate)'),
]
axes[3].legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper left', fontsize=10, frameon=True, 
               fancybox=True, shadow=True, ncol=1)

# Add verdict text
verdict_text = """VERDICT: GO:0008251 (tRNA-specific adenosine deaminase activity) is INCORRECT for squid ADAR2

Key evidence:
• Domain architecture: 3 dsRBDs + ADAR-type deaminase = classic ADAR2 (mRNA editing enzyme)
• ADAT1 has NO dsRBDs — completely different architecture
• CHAE active-site motif conserved with human ADAR2, absent in ADAT1
• Deaminase domain: 23% 3-mer similarity to human ADAR2, only 6.4% to ADAT1
• Correct term: GO:0003726 (double-stranded RNA adenosine deaminase activity)
• Failure mode: Within-superfamily mis-placement (ADAR ≠ ADAT)"""

axes[3].text(0.02, 0.55, verdict_text, transform=axes[3].transAxes, fontsize=9,
            verticalalignment='top', fontfamily='monospace',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='#FFEBEE', edgecolor='#F44336', alpha=0.8))

fig.suptitle('Domain Architecture Comparison: Squid ADAR2 vs. Human ADAR2 vs. Human ADAT1',
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=0.98)
plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.96])
plt.savefig('domain_architecture_comparison.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Figure saved as domain_architecture_comparison.png")
